creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Antoine Brodin from comment #4)
> You don't test a port on i386 with make ARCH=i386,  you test it by creating a poudriere i386 jail and running poudriere bulk on it.

I'm not sure what you wanted to say with that comment?

Is the method I used not suitable to generate the BROKEN message I wanted to see?

I did not intended to test building the port - all I wanted was to see whether I get the same BROKEN message as the originator of this PR (and I did not, since the message was displayed by "echo", not "printf").

I do test ports with "poudriere testport" on several 12.x, 13.x and -CURRENT jails for amd64 and i386 (but do not use "poudriere bulk", since I have no actual use for 12.x, 13.x, or i386 packages).
